{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Outlet Manager",
  "description": "Montaigne AH Limited popularly known as Montaigne Place is Nigeria&#39;s largest Luxury Skincare, Cosmetic, Fragrance and Well-being retail company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Outlet Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;GRA, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Lead others and work effectively with store team
	Supervise, assign and direct activities of the store&rsquo;s team
	Effectively communicate information to store team and supervisors in an open and timely manner
	Support Store team with actions plans for operational and service improvement
	Assist customers with their questions, problems and complaints
	Promote the company&#39;s customer service culture (greet, offer help, and thank)
	Provide colleagues personalized customer service feedback and coaching
	Handle all customer relations issues in accordance with company policy
	Promote a positive shopping experience for all customers
	Maintain customer privacy by not sharing customer data without authorization.
	Stock shelves
	Manage re-order levels &amp; restocking from HQ for the store
	Conduct merchandizing activities in line with approved brand guidelines
	Account for promotional and sales support materials
	Organize and execute the display and maintenance of off-shelf merchandise.


&nbsp;Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree
	3-4&nbsp;years of retail store experience.
	Experience as store or outlet manager related retail environment and/or Luxury, boutique or fashion related industry experience with a client book is strongly preferred. Experience with retail point of sale system very necessary
	Accuracy and attention to detail.
	Ability to organize and prioritize a variety of tasks/projects.
	Ability to work a full-time flexible schedule, including some early morning and weekend shifts, to work overtime as needed, and to respond to urgent issues at the store when they arise.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are to send their CV to:&nbsp;montaigneplacehr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the mail.",

Outlet Manager Job at Montaigne AH Limited




        
    

        
        Montaigne AH Limited popularly known as Montaigne Place is Nigeria's largest Luxury Skincare, Cosmetic, Fragrance and Well-being retail company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Outlet Manager
Job Location: GRA, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Lead others and work effectively with store team
	Supervise, assign and direct activities of the store’s team
	Effectively communicate information to store team and supervisors in an open and timely manner
	Support Store team with actions plans for operational and service improvement
	Assist customers with their questions, problems and complaints
	Promote the company's customer service culture (greet, offer help, and thank)
	Provide colleagues personalized customer service feedback and coaching
	Handle all customer relations issues in accordance with company policy
	Promote a positive shopping experience for all customers
	Maintain customer privacy by not sharing customer data without authorization.
	Stock shelves
	Manage re-order levels & restocking from HQ for the store
	Conduct merchandizing activities in line with approved brand guidelines
	Account for promotional and sales support materials
	Organize and execute the display and maintenance of off-shelf merchandise.


 Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree
	3-4 years of retail store experience.
	Experience as store or outlet manager related retail environment and/or Luxury, boutique or fashion related industry experience with a client book is strongly preferred. Experience with retail point of sale system very necessary
	Accuracy and attention to detail.
	Ability to organize and prioritize a variety of tasks/projects.
	Ability to work a full-time flexible schedule, including some early morning and weekend shifts, to work overtime as needed, and to respond to urgent issues at the store when they arise.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are to send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Operations Manager Job at Patrisan Jozzy Recruitment
content:
Patrisan Jozzy Recruitment is a dedicated recruitment agency specializing in connecting top talent with leading companies across various industries. We are committed to fostering successful matches that drive growth for businesses and career advancement for professionals.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing and coordinating all outlet operations to ensure efficiency, consistent quality, excellent service, achievement of targets, effective budget management, and successful launch of new outlets.


Key Responsibilities


	Multi-Outlet Operations Oversight
	Budget Management & Financial Accountability
	Sales & Target Achievement
	New Outlet Development & Expansion
	Staff Supervision & Performance Management
	Inventory & Stock Control Oversight
	Quality Control & Compliance
	Reporting & Accountability
	Process Improvement & Coordination.


Key Deliverables


	Achievement of sales and performance targets
	Effective budget management and cost control
	Successful setup and launch of new outlets
	Standardized and efficient operations
	Strong staff performance and accountability
	Accurate inventory control and bin card compliance
	Timely and accurate reporting
	High customer satisfaction and service consistency
	Compliance with operational, quality, and financial standards.


Salary
N400,000 - N600,000 per month. 

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Studio Manager",
  "description": "Glow FM Akure is a vibrant and popular radio station broadcasting from the heart of Akure, Ondo State, Nigeria. With a frequency of 99.1 FM, the station has become a household name in the region, entertaining and informing a wide audience of listeners.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Studio Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Akure, Ondo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Glow FM is seeking a competent, organised, and experienced Studio Manager to oversee the daily operations of our broadcast studio.
	The ideal candidate must understand radio studio operations, live broadcast coordination, audio quality control, studio equipment handling, presenter support, and production workflow.
	This role requires someone who can work calmly under pressure, coordinate people and equipment effectively, and ensure that programmes, news, interviews, recordings, and live sessions run smoothly.
	The Studio Manager will work closely with presenters, newscasters, producers, engineers, and the management team to maintain professional broadcast standards and support the station&rsquo;s on-air and digital media output.


Responsibilities


	Oversee the daily operation and organisation of the broadcast studio.
	Coordinate live and recorded studio sessions.
	Ensure studio equipment is properly set up before programmes, interviews, news bulletins, and recordings.
	Monitor sound quality, audio levels, microphones, mixer settings, and studio signal flow.
	Support OAPs, newscasters, guests, producers, and technical staff during live broadcasts.
	Operate or supervise the use of studio consoles, microphones, playback systems, recording tools, and related equipment.
	Work with the Engineering Department to report, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues quickly.
	Ensure smooth handover between programmes, news bulletins, live shows, and recorded segments.
	Maintain order, discipline, and professionalism inside the studio.
	Coordinate studio bookings, programme timing, guest readiness, and production schedules.
	Support recording, editing, packaging, and archiving of broadcast materials when required.
	Help improve studio workflow, broadcast quality, and operational efficiency.


Requirements


	OND, HND, Degree, or relevant technical/media qualification is an added advantage, but practical experience is very important.
	Previous experience in radio, broadcasting, studio operations, sound production, or media production.
	Strong understanding of radio studio workflow and live broadcast operations.
	Ability to operate or supervise studio equipment such as microphones, mixers, headphones, playback systems, recording software, and audio routing tools.
	Good knowledge of audio quality, sound balance, signal flow, and basic troubleshooting.
	Leadership, coordination, and communication skills.
	Ability to work calmly under pressure, especially during live programmes.
	Must be disciplined, punctual, organised, and detail-oriented.
	Must be able to work flexible shifts when required.
	Basic knowledge of Adobe Audition, audio editing software, radio automation tools, or livestreaming tools is an added advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;dokita2010@gmail.com&nbsp;&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-03",

Studio Manager Job at Glow FM




        
    

        
        Glow FM Akure is a vibrant and popular radio station broadcasting from the heart of Akure, Ondo State, Nigeria. With a frequency of 99.1 FM, the station has become a household name in the region, entertaining and informing a wide audience of listeners.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Studio Manager
Job Location: Akure, Ondo
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Glow FM is seeking a competent, organised, and experienced Studio Manager to oversee the daily operations of our broadcast studio.
	The ideal candidate must understand radio studio operations, live broadcast coordination, audio quality control, studio equipment handling, presenter support, and production workflow.
	This role requires someone who can work calmly under pressure, coordinate people and equipment effectively, and ensure that programmes, news, interviews, recordings, and live sessions run smoothly.
	The Studio Manager will work closely with presenters, newscasters, producers, engineers, and the management team to maintain professional broadcast standards and support the station’s on-air and digital media output.


Responsibilities


	Oversee the daily operation and organisation of the broadcast studio.
	Coordinate live and recorded studio sessions.
	Ensure studio equipment is properly set up before programmes, interviews, news bulletins, and recordings.
	Monitor sound quality, audio levels, microphones, mixer settings, and studio signal flow.
	Support OAPs, newscasters, guests, producers, and technical staff during live broadcasts.
	Operate or supervise the use of studio consoles, microphones, playback systems, recording tools, and related equipment.
	Work with the Engineering Department to report, troubleshoot, and resolve technical issues quickly.
	Ensure smooth handover between programmes, news bulletins, live shows, and recorded segments.
	Maintain order, discipline, and professionalism inside the studio.
	Coordinate studio bookings, programme timing, guest readiness, and production schedules.
	Support recording, editing, packaging, and archiving of broadcast materials when required.
	Help improve studio workflow, broadcast quality, and operational efficiency.


Requirements


	OND, HND, Degree, or relevant technical/media qualification is an added advantage, but practical experience is very important.
	Previous experience in radio, broadcasting, studio operations, sound production, or media production.
	Strong understanding of radio studio workflow and live broadcast operations.
	Ability to operate or supervise studio equipment such as microphones, mixers, headphones, playback systems, recording software, and audio routing tools.
	Good knowledge of audio quality, sound balance, signal flow, and basic troubleshooting.
	Leadership, coordination, and communication skills.
	Ability to work calmly under pressure, especially during live programmes.
	Must be disciplined, punctual, organised, and detail-oriented.
	Must be able to work flexible shifts when required.
	Basic knowledge of Adobe Audition, audio editing software, radio automation tools, or livestreaming tools is an added advantage.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]  using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "description": "Montaigne AH Limited popularly known as Montaigne Place is Nigeria&#39;s largest Luxury Skincare, Cosmetic, Fragrance and Well-being retail company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Spa Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;GRA, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure excellent customer service
	Design Spa procedure manual and ensure strict adherence by alL Spa team members
	Provide Consultation services to clients
	Train all staff members on new products and treatmets
	Suggest Value-added services to management to maximize sales
	Demonstrate indepth knowlege of in-store retail products/brands
	Oversee the operation and maintainance of all Spa facilities and equipment


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in management or related fields
	At least 3 years in a supervisory/managerial capacity
	Strong leadership &amp; organizational skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are to submit their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;montaigneplacehr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the mail.",

Spa Manager Job at Montaigne AH Limited




        
    

        
        Montaigne AH Limited popularly known as Montaigne Place is Nigeria's largest Luxury Skincare, Cosmetic, Fragrance and Well-being retail company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Spa Manager
Job Location: GRA, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Ensure excellent customer service
	Design Spa procedure manual and ensure strict adherence by alL Spa team members
	Provide Consultation services to clients
	Train all staff members on new products and treatmets
	Suggest Value-added services to management to maximize sales
	Demonstrate indepth knowlege of in-store retail products/brands
	Oversee the operation and maintainance of all Spa facilities and equipment


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in management or related fields
	At least 3 years in a supervisory/managerial capacity
	Strong leadership & organizational skills.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are to submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Maintenance Officer",
  "description": "Carlson Hotel, a four-star hotel located at Ikoyi, Lagos,&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties


	Preventive Maintenance: Scheduling and performing regular checks on HVAC systems, boilers, water systems, and electrical components to prevent breakdowns.
	Guest Room and Facility Repairs: Fixing broken items (furniture, faucets, locks), painting, repairing drywall, and responding to work orders generated by housekeeping or guests.
	Safety and Compliance: Testing fire alarms, sprinkler systems, emergency lighting, and ensuring all areas comply with local health and safety regulations.
	Systems Maintenance: Operating and maintaining major machinery, including elevators, laundry equipment, kitchen appliances, and swimming pool filtration systems.
	Energy Management: Monitoring energy consumption and optimizing equipment performance to reduce operating costs.
	Exterior Upkeep: Maintaining the building facade, parking lot, lighting, and landscaping.
	Emergency Response: Acting immediately to resolve emergency situations, such as power outages or water leaks.
	Inventory and Contractor Management: Tracking maintenance supplies and overseeing external contractors for specialized repairs.


Salary
N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;info@carlson-hotel.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, contact 0908 637 0687 (whatsapp only).",

Maintenance Officer Job at Carlson Hotel




        
    

        
        Carlson Hotel, a four-star hotel located at Ikoyi, Lagos, recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Maintenance Officer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Preventive Maintenance: Scheduling and performing regular checks on HVAC systems, boilers, water systems, and electrical components to prevent breakdowns.
	Guest Room and Facility Repairs: Fixing broken items (furniture, faucets, locks), painting, repairing drywall, and responding to work orders generated by housekeeping or guests.
	Safety and Compliance: Testing fire alarms, sprinkler systems, emergency lighting, and ensuring all areas comply with local health and safety regulations.
	Systems Maintenance: Operating and maintaining major machinery, including elevators, laundry equipment, kitchen appliances, and swimming pool filtration systems.
	Energy Management: Monitoring energy consumption and optimizing equipment performance to reduce operating costs.
	Exterior Upkeep: Maintaining the building facade, parking lot, lighting, and landscaping.
	Emergency Response: Acting immediately to resolve emergency situations, such as power outages or water leaks.
	Inventory and Contractor Management: Tracking maintenance supplies and overseeing external contractors for specialized repairs.


Salary
N100,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquiries, contact 0908 637 0687 (whatsapp only).    

    

        Click here to Apply
